summary.intent: Implement tasks #297, #298, and #299 — the framework-agnostic core of 8 color components (ColorSwatch, ColorField, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, AngleSlider, ColorSwatchPicker) plus a shared ColorValue type in ars-core, all in one combined PR.
summary.outcome: All 8 components implemented, CI green (all 22 checks pass including coverage), PR #698 opened with Closes #297/#298/#299. A subsequent Codex review surfaced 8 behavioral bugs (stale props, percentage parsing, NaN parsing, stale drag value, missing tabstop, missing perceptual label, readonly bypass, disabled hidden input) which were all fixed TDD and pushed in a second commit.

repo: core_maths::CoreFloat is the workspace's established no_std float tool (used in ars-i18n); must call methods in fully-qualified form CoreFloat::powf(x, exp). CoreFloat lacks min/max — require private fmin/fmax helpers.
MessageFn only has From<F> impls for a fixed set of function signatures. Each new message signature (e.g. Fn(&ColorNameParts, &Locale)) requires a hand-written From<F> impl in message_fn.rs.

Part variants with f64 fields cannot use #[derive(ComponentPart)] since f64 is not Eq/Hash. Requires manual ComponentPart, PartialEq, Eq (via to_bits), Hash impls — see input/slider/mod.rs for the precedent.

Bindable::get() returns the controlled prop value; set() updates only the internal value. Change-end callbacks must use the new Bindable::pending() to get the in-flight drag value rather than get(), otherwise on_change_end fires with the stale controlled value.

Machine components must implement on_props_changed + props_output_changed (mirroring input/slider) to re-emit SyncValue/SetProps events when the parent changes controlled props, otherwise controlled value/disabled/readonly updates go stale until remount.

has_description flag pattern: components with a Description child slot need a SetHasDescription(bool) event (fired by the adapter on child mount/unmount) — without it the aria-describedby branch is permanently dead code.
CssProperty::Custom(name) renders as --{name}, so passing "--ars-swatch-color" would double the dashes. Pass "ars-swatch-color".

CI native coverage uses cargo +nightly llvm-cov nextest --branch --workspace (not -p); --workspace runs cross-crate integration tests that boost per-crate branch coverage by ~1.1% vs a -p-only run. Match this exactly when debugging coverage thresholds locally.

cargo xci-fast snapshot-count gate requires ≥1 snapshot per state variant. A 3-state machine (Idle/Dragging/Focused) failing with snapshots=2, state_variants=3 means one state has no snapshot coverage.

spec-compile-snippets compiles spec code blocks in isolation; most are no_check fenced. Spec code blocks frequently reference non-existent APIs and diverge from the actual codebase — treat spec as authoritative contract, not compiling source of truth.

Workspace denies clippy::suspicious. Stable 1.96 added unnecessary_option_map_or_else to that group, surfacing a pre-existing lint in pagination/mod.rs that blocks CI for everyone on the updated toolchain.

code.finding: parse_color_string accepted hsl(NaN,...)/hsl(inf,...) because parse::<f64>() succeeds on those strings. ColorValue::new only debug_asserts finiteness, so release builds silently store NaN in styles and ARIA attrs. Fix: parse_finite_f64 helper that returns None on !is_finite().

format_value renders saturation/lightness/alpha as percentages (e.g. '50') but commit_input parsed the raw string and clamped to channel_range [0,1], so typing '50' in a percentage field silently changed the value to 1.0. Fix: channel_is_percentage helper + divide by 100 before clamping in commit_input.

Props::readonly only set aria-readonly on the input but not HtmlAttr::ReadOnly, and the machine still accepted Change events and blur-commits while readonly. Fix: add HtmlAttr::ReadOnly to input_attrs and early-return None on Change/blur-commit when props.readonly.

focused_index is None on initial render; every item got tabindex=-1, making the widget unreachable via keyboard. Fix: tabbable_index helper returns selected index (or 0 as fallback) for the idle roving tabindex.

aria-valuetext only rendered two raw channel values (e.g. '75%, 40%') without a perceptual color name. Fix: thread color_name_parts through value_text MessageFn (new 5-arg signature Fn(f64,f64,&str,&str,&Locale)) and update the MessageFn From impl.

DragEnd ChangeEnd effect read ctx.value.get() which returns the controlled prop (stale until parent re-renders) instead of the just-computed drag value. Fix: use ctx.value.pending() — the new Bindable accessor that returns the internal in-flight value.

Hidden form input was not disabled when the control was disabled, allowing it to submit a value on form submit. Fix: mirror props.disabled onto HtmlAttr::Disabled in hidden_input_attrs. Same pattern needed across color_area, color_wheel, angle_slider, color_field, color_swatch_picker.

Bindable semantics: controlled mode set() updates internal field only; get() returns the controlled prop from Props. Added pending() method that returns &internal — the only way to access the in-flight drag value before the parent acknowledges the change.

workflow: TDD discipline caught all 8 Codex review findings cleanly: write a failing test that names the bug, then implement the fix. Avoids regressions and gives reviewers confidence.
zsh has several read-only built-in variable names (status, ERRNO, etc.) that silently shadow user variables or crash scripts. Use non-colliding names like cov_state in background poll scripts.
When measuring branch coverage locally to match CI, always use the exact CI flags (--workspace, not -p). Per-package runs can be ~1.1% lower than CI due to missing cross-crate test contributions.
Spec code blocks in this project describe intent, not compilable source. Treat them as a contract spec, not a direct implementation guide — verify every API exists before using it.
The waiting-for-codex-review skill drives a full TDD→fix→push→re-trigger cycle. Codex review findings should be treated as P1/P2 bugs and addressed with the same rigor as the original implementation.

friction: The spec described multiple non-existent APIs (use_platform_effects(), track_pointer_drag(), Callback<ColorValue>, generate_id()) requiring significant reconciliation before implementation could start.
core_maths CoreFloat lacks min/max despite providing other transcendental methods, requiring private fmin/fmax workaround helpers.
Codex review was rate-limited with 'You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews' on both trigger attempts, blocking the required 👍 approval and stalling the merge workflow.
Background watch scripts crashed due to zsh read-only 'status' built-in variable, requiring a relaunch with a renamed variable.
Local branch coverage measurement (-p) is ~1.1% lower than CI's --workspace measurement, causing false alarm debugging sessions before identifying the discrepancy.

Stable Rust 1.96 (released mid-session) introduced unnecessary_option_map_or_else into clippy::suspicious which is workspace-denied, surfacing a pre-existing lint in pagination/mod.rs as a CI blocker unrelated to the task.
Type inference E0282 on TransitionPlan context_only closures required annotating every |ctx| as |ctx: &mut Context| across all early-return blocks in the color_field machine.
ColorField's has_description flag was silently dead code (no setter event existed) — only caught during the coverage-fix pass, not during initial implementation review.
open_items: Codex review (👍) is still pending due to rate limit exhaustion — merge is blocked until quota resets and a clean pass is obtained.
Framework-specific adapter implementations (Leptos/Dioxus color component adapters) are explicitly out of scope for this PR and remain as future tasks.
ColorValue/shared color types live in color-picker.md §Types but could be promoted to spec/shared/color-types.md — deferred as orthogonal to implementation correctness.

Proptest suites for color components are marked #[ignore] (nightly-only) and not in the fast CI path; they exercise invariants not covered by unit tests but require a dedicated nightly proptest run to surface failures.
Issues #297/#298/#299 auto-close on merge but the merge itself is the user's decision after Codex 👍 — not yet done.
